_doc: The data-practices register — what every wired harness hook COLLECTS, what it USES that for, where the data GOES, and whether its own firing is WITNESSED (recorded). bdo, 2026-06-20: 'account for all activity, even Claude hooks like session start and tool call, and start auditing their data collection and usage for a shared gateway.' This is the declared half; loop/activity.py reconciles it against the live .claude/settings.json wiring and REFUSES an undeclared collector (a wired hook absent here) or a ghost (an entry no longer wired) — done-line 0143. The 'collects' field names the data each hook reads; 'witnessed' is whether the hook records THAT IT FIRED (most are false today — that gap is what organ 2, the runtime witness, closes). Authored declaration, version-controlled, human-legible; a new entry is added by the session that wires a new hook, never silently.

wiring_sources.claude: .claude/settings.json — the Claude Code hook wiring; this layer is the fold's floor and its teeth bite here
wiring_sources.codex: .codex/hooks.json — the rendered Codex layer; reconciled only if present (deferred, done-line 0143 not-in-scope)
wiring_sources.prototype: built but not yet wired into any settings file — declared so it cannot be wired as a silent collector later

command_guard.uses_for: deny raw git/gh mutations (fence registry); watch every other external command for wrapper-pressure

spawn_guard.uses_for: gate branded node-spawns against the trust rung; surface an unbranded spawn once per session

write_guard.uses_for: a new file lands only under a governing CLAUDE.md; a penned-dir write must be a carbon copy

freeze_guard.uses_for: refuse any in-place edit/overwrite of a frozen done-line

change_guard.uses_for: refuse a change that breaks a declared change-policy invariant

workstation_guard.uses_for: refuse a write/edit whose target path is in a worktree (or the viewport) other than the session's own — the workstation write-fence (done-line 0147); record the denied attempt so the trespass shame beat witnesses it

trespass_shame.uses_for: shame a session for ATTEMPTING to flip the viewport or write a foreign tree (even though the fence denied it); grows louder per new attempt, quiet when it stops

summon.uses_for: hand the session its open summons, owner backlog, and top gap ambiently

heartbeat.uses_for: run one guaranteed setpoint-bounded tick of the level-triggered loop so atoms flow toward value-confirm (the in-harness driver, done-line 0178); tick-only and fail-open in hook mode — never drains, never fake-stamps (the mock-human valve is shut)
heartbeat.sink: .ai-native/log/ (a tick admission + pipeline events/receipts when the beat advances work) + stdout

git:sync.uses_for: fast-forward the viewport to origin/main; restore a stranded viewport when its work is safe

git:garden.uses_for: report worktree chores and stranded branches; cut only provably-merged clean branches

gesture_surface.uses_for: surface pending owner gestures (realness / rung / policy confirms) at session open

standing_surface.uses_for: show the standing external surface and the delta since last poll

reflect_auto.uses_for: apply what enabled reflection rules name to registered external surfaces, every act receipted
reflect_auto.sink: via the reflect pen -> the log (receipts)

session_register.uses_for: record this session into the watch registry so the external watcher can resume an idle one (done-line 0135)

idle_reminder.uses_for: softly inject a gateway-bounded continue reminder after an idle gap (the retired continue-beat block's replacement)

mock_shame.uses_for: scream every effective mock, louder the longer the set sits

owner_ask_shame.uses_for: scream every unsurfaced owner-ask, louder the longer it sits

probe_codex.uses_for: record the undocumented Codex hook contract to design the Codex watcher against real readings
